steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers concluded that the trait's prevalence varies systematically with absolute latitude across all avian orders. The correct graph must directly show that prevalence rises as latitude increases.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Charadriiforms unaffected by latitude: Contradicts the finding by claiming latitude does not affect one order. ✗
  • Choice B — no clear prevalence rates: Fails to display the actual percentage values needed to confirm the latitude–prevalence relationship. ✗
  • Choice C — prevalence rises from 34% at 0–30° to 60% at 50–80°: Directly shows increasing prevalence with latitude across orders. ✓
  • Choice D — trait distribution by location only: Shows where the trait appears but not how prevalent it is at each latitude band. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
